data.body: PASS 1 review — T3 Integration feasibility [overlay]: NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS. This plan is an internal observability sweep (migrate diagnostic stderr prints to the named logger; bring broad-excepts to the observability floor) against an already-established in-repo convention (src/rebar/_logging.py). It integrates no external API/CLI/service/library and asserts no previously-unused capability — it relies on the stdlib `logging` module, the existing `_logging` substrate, and the existing `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout machine contract that the reconciler `json.loads` (an internal, already-used contract). Per the T3 overlay gate and ANTI-FP rule ("an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable"), no T3 findings. No external subcommands/endpoints or auth/HTTPS preconditions are in scope.
